stemHtml: During the approach, the horizontal stabiliser becomes jammed in position. The aircraft's fly-by-wire flight controls will automatically switch to direct law so the aeroplane has to be flown completely manually for a short time before landing. Whilst on the approach, the fly-by-wire flight controls automatically maintain the correct pitch attitude by applying a continuous pitch-up command to the elevators. What will the effect be on control authority of changing to direct law just before landing?

options.a: The autopilot maintains the pitch attitude even in direct law.
options.b: The pilot will have the most control authority in this law, except during a go-around.
options.c: The approach will be easier for the pilots to fly in direct law due to better control authority.
options.d: The control authority will be decreased in both directions.
